messages.text: Team, we need to make a call on the housing material for the motor controller enclosure. We're at DVT and the current aluminum design is 340g over our weight target.
I ran the FEA on magnesium AZ91D. It meets structural requirements at 0.6mm wall thickness. Weight savings: 290g. That puts us 50g under target.
Heads up — switching to magnesium means we need a different die caster. Our current vendor (Proto Labs) doesn't do mag. I have quotes from Dynacast but lead time is 8 weeks vs 4 for aluminum.
8 weeks puts us right at the DVT build deadline. Can we get Dynacast to expedite? What's the cost delta?
Expedite is possible at +15% tooling cost ($4,200 premium). I can get us 6 weeks. Still tight but doable if we lock the CAD by Friday.
Decision: we're going with magnesium AZ91D for the motor controller housing. @elena please lock in Dynacast with expedite. I'll have final STEP files by Thursday EOD.
Thermal sim results for motor controller rev D are in. Bad news: junction temp hits 142°C at sustained load. Target is 125°C. The passive heat sink is not cutting it.
142°C is a hard fail for the MOSFET ratings. We can't ship DVT units with that margin. What are our options?

Option A: Redesign heat sink with fins (adds 45g, 2 weeks). Option B: Add a 25mm fan (adds 15g, $2.30 BOM, 1 week). Option C: Reduce max continuous power by 20% (software limit, 0 weeks but degrades performance).
From the drivetrain side: Option C is a non-starter. We sized the motor for the current power spec. 20% reduction means we can't hold payload at max speed on inclines.

Schedule can't absorb 2 weeks for Option A. Let's go with Option B — the fan. @maya can you update the thermal spec from passive to active cooling and revise the enclosure CAD to accommodate the fan mount?

Spec change logged: motor controller thermal management changed from passive heat sink to active cooling (25mm fan). Updated thermal target: junction temp < 105°C at sustained load with fan at 5000 RPM. I'll update the enclosure CAD by Wednesday.
DVT integration status: the LIDAR driver firmware is blocked. We found a timing issue in the SPI bus initialization that causes intermittent data corruption at > 40°C ambient. No workaround yet.
This blocks the DVT integration test suite. @james what's the timeline to root cause?

We've isolated it to a clock domain crossing issue. Fix is straightforward but needs validation across temperature range. Best case: 3 business days. Worst case: 1 week if we find additional edge cases.
Does this affect the chassis sensor mounting? The LIDAR bracket was designed for the current board outline. If the fix requires a board respin that changes dimensions, I need to know ASAP.
No board respin needed — this is a pure firmware fix. Board outline and mounting holes stay the same. The bracket design is safe.

OK. Action items: (1) @james prioritize the SPI fix, target Wednesday delivery. (2) I'm pushing the DVT integration test suite to next Monday. (3) All workstream leads: flag any downstream dependencies on sensor data by EOD today.
